profile.name: Calder Wong / CJ
profile.title: AI Systems Builder, Knowledge Architect, and Hapa Operator
profile.summary: A professional character sheet that turns what was consumed, learned, practiced, built, and routed through agents into a fast, evidence-backed portfolio.
profile.resume_summary: Builds local-first AI knowledge systems that convert source material, AI turns, Hapa nodes, media artifacts, protocols, and board state into inspectable professional capability. Strongest indexed families include Media Pipeline / Video Production, Hapa Wiki Curation, Schema / Data Modeling, Asset / Attachment Management, Data Hygiene / Staleness Detection.

claims.title: Turns lived as applied skill evidence
claims.body: AI collaboration turns are not treated as chat debris. They are indexed as proof of practice, result lineage, and reusable capability.

claims.title: Knowledge intake becomes operational memory
claims.body: Books, media, references, tools, and source systems are normalized into topic, entity, and skill projections for retrieval.

claims.title: Hapa nodes convert learning into apps
claims.body: Capabilities are framed as using and enhancing node skills, then linked back to general skill evidence, cards, topics, and artifacts.

outcomes.title: Built a Second Brain projection stack
outcomes.meta: SQLite, CLI, API, wiki, node maps
outcomes.body: A local-first memory system that turns consumed material, AI turns, Hapa apps, media, and protocols into queryable skill and result lineage.

outcomes.title: Designed protocol-aware Hapa apps
outcomes.body: Hapa nodes carry standard contracts so humans and agents can inspect, run, extend, and track them with predictable surfaces.

outcomes.title: Compressed professional proof into game UX
outcomes.body: The Character Sheet reframes portfolio proof as RPG-readable stats without losing the evidence trail a professional evaluator needs.

outcomes.title: Mapped agents and harnesses to work
outcomes.body: Agents are represented as operating profiles with skills, runtime affordances, memory policy, and node-level capability bridges.

outcomes.title: Created media lineage for protocol artifacts
outcomes.body: Generated images, cards, avatars, and queue records are connected to node skills, turn skills, targets, and protocol presentation roles.

outcomes.title: Tracked implementation as board state
outcomes.body: The Character Sheet is already represented as an Overwatch Kanban board with source-labeled tasks and acceptance criteria.

proof_lanes.label: Knowledge
proof_lanes.body: Source systems, content items, exposures, topic profiles, chunks, and entities explain where capability came from.

proof_lanes.label: Practice
proof_lanes.body: Turns, learning links, result links, and wisdom cards show how knowledge was applied during AI collaboration.

proof_lanes.label: Output
proof_lanes.body: Nodes, capabilities, bridges, protocol docs, media records, and board cards show what the work produced.

featured_nodes.label: hapa-dev-proto
featured_nodes.description: # hapa-dev-proto: The Hapa Protocol Workbench The `hapa-dev-proto` repository represents the core desktop environment of the Hapa Operating System. It functions as a local-first, highly sovereign developer workbench and agentic orchestrator that unites cloud APIs, local silicon inference, card-based knowledge ingestion, spatial visualizations, and decentralized peer-to-peer data distribution....
